observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:

Enjoin the management to adhere to the provisions of RA No. 7160 regarding the reversion of unexpended balances of MOOE and the proper classification of budgetary items as continuing appropriations;

Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to immediately revert the remaining balance of MOOE under continuing appropriations for CY 2023 to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund; and

Require the Municipal Budget Office, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to present only budget amounts for CO under continuing appropriations and ensure that the amount presented in the SCBAA corroborate with the existing records by using the formula for computation for proper check and balance.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2023-page 51 | There were still MOOE items presented under the continuing appropriations.





Reversion of the MOOE under continuing appropriations to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund was not yet made.



The CO under continuing appropriations did not still tally with the computation based on the existing records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to:
 
Submit the lacking documents as enumerated in the lists provided by the Audit Team.

Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to ensure compliance with the documentary requirements to enable the Audit Team to evaluate projects completely, establish the validity, legitimacy and propriety of the transaction.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2023-page 64 | Management had partially submitted the lacking documents.




Some required documents attached to the disbursement vouchers for the payment of infrastructure projects were not attached.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the HRMO to ensure completeness and timely submission to the Municipal Accountant of the needed documents for the liquidation of payroll such as DTRs, Accomplishment Reports of Job Order personnel, and attendance of the SB to facilitate swift processing of documents for the liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2023-page 69 | No action was taken by management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

Closely coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan in facilitating the revision and update of the San Jacinto Local Market Code. This should specifically incorporate the lease term in the Contract of Lease, and updated market stall rates to reflect current economic considerations;

Direct the Market Inspector to ensure that contracts were prepared and executed to every stallholder, duly notarized, and on file; and 

Direct the Market Inspector to initiate an inspection aimed to identify the actual occupants of the stalls as compared to the Municipality’s list of stallholders for the imposition of sanctions and implementation of Section 6 of the Contract of Lease and Section 15 of the Market Code.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2023-page 87 | No action was taken by management.










No action was taken by management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Direct the Market Board, under the initiative of the Municipal Mayor, serving as the Chairman, to enforce the provision outlined in Section 14 of the San Jacinto Market Code and Section 3 of the Contract of Lease which provides the imposition of a 5% penalty in the monthly rentals and the cancellation or revocation of the lease contract or award on failure to pay the rental fee for three consecutive months;

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Market Inspector and with the guidance and supervision of the Municipal Mayor, to formulate effective collection strategies and ensure their implementation accordingly; and

To include specific provision/s related to collection policies, stating thereof the due process in the collection of delinquent market stall rentals, during the updating or revision of the Market Code.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2023-page 91 | No action was taken by management.










No action was taken by management.


No action was taken by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to monitor and follow up on the timely liquidation of cash advances by preparing and sending demand letters as needed.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2023-page 107 | Demand letters were not sent by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Direct the municipal vehicle drivers to complete the Driver’s Trip Ticket before issuing approved gasoline requisition slips;

Ensure that the Municipal Accountant verifies that claims for the payment of gasoline, oil and lubricants are supported by properly filled-out driver’s trip tickets to assess the validity and reasonableness of fuel consumption; and

Assign personnel responsible for overseeing transportation and fuel management to compile and submit these reports to the office of the Auditor, as required by COA Circular No. 77-61, for proper monitoring and control of government expenditures related to vehicle operations.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2023- page 108 | Management ensured that the Driver’s Trip Tickets were fully accomplished before issuing approved gasoline requisition slips.




The Municipal Accountant required properly filled-out driver’s trip tickets before payments were made.




No actions taken by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to collect business taxes from outside contractors who engage in projects within the municipality applying the situs of taxation. In this regard, issue demand letters from these contractors or require them to pay the accrued business taxes with the municipal government before being allowed to participate in the bidding process.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2023-page 110 | No action was taken by management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Chairperson of the GAD Focal Point System-TWG to:

Incorporate only GAD-related programs and activities into the GAD Plan and Budget based on gender responsiveness that address specific gender issues and concerns affecting the welfare and full development of women.

Determine the reasons for the non-implementation and underutilization of GAD PPAs in 2023 and ensure that future GAD PPAs indicated in the GPB will be fully implemented and utilized to maximize the allocated GAD budget.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2023- page 118 | No action was taken by management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

Reconcile the recorded balances of the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF and the Cash in Bank-LCCA account under the Trust Fund, and make the necessary adjustments with corresponding supporting documents; and

Maintain subsidiary ledgers for each year to facilitate the monitoring of the balances of the Trust Liabilites-LDRRMF, which should be disclosed in the Notes to the  Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2023- page 123 | No action was taken by management.




No action was taken by management.

Inventory Committee was not functioning as intended. Management committed to create new Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical count of PPE.
The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant have yet to submit proof of preparation and maintenance of property records for all PPE.
The SLs prepared were for current year only and not for all accounts.
Inventory count of all PPE remained incomplete. Management committed to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason:
Inventory Committee was not functioning as intended. Management committed to create new Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical count of PPE.
The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant have yet to submit proof of preparation and maintenance of property records for all PPE.
The SLs prepared were for current year only and not for all accounts.
Inventory count of all PPE remained incomplete. Management committed to comply with the recommendation.

observations.managementAction:
The LCE has issued directive to the Mun. Accountant to issue demand letters to officers and employees with unliquidated Cash Advances.
Locate and serve demand letters to officers and employees with unliquidated Cash Advances. All cash advances for 2024 have already been liquidated except for a couple of advances. A majority number of officers and employees with unliquidated Cash Advance have already been served a demand letter. Several cash advances remained unliquidated despite the demand letters issued by the Municipal Accountant in the previous year.
Monitor list of Officers & Employees that have an outstanding Cash Advance and avoid granting additional cash advance to the same.
Already conducting verification, analysis, and validation of all dormant accounts. Comply with the requirements to facilitate the derecognition Unliquidated Cash Advance/ dormant Accounts.
observations.reason: Difficulty of locating officers & employees who were already resigned or retired. Some are already deceased. Management committed to comply with the recommendation and to submit request for write-off; no request for write-off was submitted by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: 15. We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to strengthen accounting controls over liabilities by conducting reconciliation of records, reviewing thoroughly the schedule of payables due to concerned agencies during each month and ensuring that remittances are based on the actual payroll deductions. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant carefully analyze every transaction before recording them in the books and assign personnel to review the entries made to each subsidiary account with negative balances and effect the necessary adjusting entries.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant's review of inter-agency payable accounts has revealed errors in GSIS billings. Specifically, premium contributions were billed for employees no longer working for the LGU. To resolve this, Management plans to coordinate with GSIS to update their records, removing these former employees from their database. This reconciliation will correct the overbilling and eliminate the resulting negative balance in the LGU's accounts.
The process of reconciliation has been hampered due to lack of supporting documentation from previous Accountant, making it difficult to pinpoint the reasons for the negative balances in these accounts. Despite this challenge, he committed to continuing the reconciliation process. Any discrepancies or errors discovered during the ongoing reconciliation will be adjusted immediately to ensure a timely, accurate and reliable financial records.
observations.reason: Difficulty in tracing back transactions which had already caused the negative balances due to absence of correct documents. Reconciliation is still on-going particularly those which transpire several years ago.

observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Administrator/ LCE shall call for a meeting for the purpose of organizing the inventory committee members and their corresponding role.
The Municipal Engineer did not provide the Inventory Committee with the necessary data resulting to non-preparation and non-submission of RPCLRN.
The Municipal Treasurer did not prepare and maintain separate SLs and both MT and Municipal Accountant did not maintain separate LRNLCs and LRNPCs for local road projects. Also, the MT did not prepare and submit RLRN and Local Road Inventory and Road Map. Lastly, no lapsing schedule was prepared by the Municipal Accountant.
Management committed to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: The Municipal Engineer did not provide the Inventory Committee with data; lack of personnel and coordination; MT and Municipal Accountant did not maintain separate records; no lapsing schedule prepared. Management committed to comply.

observations.recommendation: 31. We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Accounting Office all the DVs, official receipts including the corresponding reports of each month on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month and the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Office of the Auditor the paid DVs on or before the 10th day of the ensuing month. Also, require the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the unsubmitted paid DVs with complete supporting documents and the original copies of the cancelled checks.
observations.managementAction:
Delays were still incurred in the submission of the accounts of the Municipality to the Audit Team, however, it was noted that improvements were made regarding the submission of accounts such that reports were already submitted on a quarterly basis with only a few days/months of delay and not submitted in bulk.
The Municipal Treasurer was directed to submit DVs and receipts timely; Municipal Accountant committed to submit unsubmitted paid DVs with complete supporting documents.
observations.reason: Delays were still incurred in submission of documents; lacking supporting documents for some DVs. Improvements noted but not full compliance.

observations.managementAction:
The LCE has already instructed the Municipal Administrator to hold such meeting for creating the inventory committee.
The Mun. Treasurer and Mun. Accountant to implement perpetual inventory system; MT to submit SSMI; MT to conduct physical count; MT and Municipal Accountant to maintain and reconcile stock cards and SLCs.
Management promised to comply with the recommendations though some actions remain not implemented due to lack of knowledgeable personnel and understaffing.
observations.reason: Laxity in complying with audit recommendation; lack of knowledgeable personnel in preparation of required records/reports; insufficiency of manpower; absence of stock cards and SLCs for reconciliation.

observations.managementAction:
Management to allocate budget and facilitate titling; Management to direct personnel to reconcile accounting records with land records; Management to submit lacking documentary requirements. No titles/documents were submitted to the audit team. No reconciliation was made. Management reminded personnel to reconcile the accounting records to actual land owned by LGU.
Management committed to expedite titling process.
observations.reason: No titles/documents were submitted; lack of reconciliation; lacking documentary requirements not submitted; Management committed to comply.

observations.recommendation: 76. Submit the lacking supporting documents for each transaction.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to submit the lacking supporting documents. Some lacking supporting documents were not yet submitted.
observations.reason: Some of lacking supporting documents were not yet submitted. Management committed to comply.

observations.recommendation:
82. Require the BAC Secretariat to submit all advance copies of perfected contracts, POs and all their required supporting documents to the Audit Team within the prescribed period; and
83. Instruct the GSO or the personnel responsible for accepting deliveries to: i) notify COA of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries for ocular verification of the existence and condition of supplies, materials and equipment; ii) submit copies of completely filled out AIRs within 24 hours upon acceptance; and iii) submit report on deliveries received, issued to end-users and remaining balance as of December 31, 2022.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to submit all perfected contracts and POs with supporting documents to COA. The GSO to notify COA of scheduled deliveries for ocular verification and submit AIRs and delivery reports. Some perfected contracts and POs were not submitted; Management committed to cause timely submission.
observations.reason: Not all perfected contracts nor POs with their supporting documents were submitted. Lack of personnel to facilitate submission; some documents sat idle waiting for a designated responsible person.

observations.recommendation: 84. Prepare and issue a well-defined set of policies and guidelines governing the implementation of AICS program which must be aligned with the guidelines issued by the DSWD governing the grants of AICS. The policies and guidelines must contain the following: · Kind of financial assistance (burial, transportation, medical, educational, food, shelter and others); · Limitations on the amounts given per kind of financial assistance; · Criteria in granting the financial assistance and the documentary requirements for each particular case; and · Well defined roles and responsibilities of the officials processing requests.
observations.managementAction: Management to prepare and issue policies and guidelines aligned with DSWD MC No. 11 s.2019. No policies were prepared and issued for the AICS program.
observations.reason: No policies and guidelines were prepared and issued. Management disregarded recommendation and no action was taken.

observations.recommendation: 85. We recommended that Management require the concerned official to provide the Auditor at the beginning of the year a list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year to enable the former to monitor the status of its implementation and assess the Agency’s compliance to transparent and full disclosure governance.
observations.managementAction: No Management action taken.
observations.reason: Management disregarded the recommendation and was not acted upon.

observations.recommendation:
86. Stop the practice of granting claims for procured goods and services on a reimbursement basis. Procurement of goods, infrastructure projects and consultancy services must pass through the regular mode of transparent and competitive bidding pursuant to RA No. 9184; and
87. Ensure that only items included in the APP will be purchased, or if there is any revision thereat, a supplemental investment program indicating additional items to be procured, must be prepared.
observations.managementAction:
Management tried to refrain from procurement on reimbursement basis, but due to extreme urgency, being an island municipality and for practical reasons, reimbursement was resorted to. Nevertheless, procurement on reimbursement basis was lessened.
No Supplemental APP was prepared when revisions occurred.
observations.reason: Inability of concerned personnel to prepare and submit the Supplemental APP; emergency circumstances and logistical constraints in an island municipality led to some reimbursements.

observations.recommendation:
88. Strictly comply with the rules and regulations on fuel consumption to minimize wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures through usage of RIS or its equivalent, and properly accomplished driver’s trip ticket in order to ensure that government vehicles are used reasonably and to avoid audit suspension or disallowance thereof;
89. Prepare and submit Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Fuel Consumption Report of LGU vehicles and heavy equipment to the Commission on Audit (thru Municipal Accountant) for verification and evaluation of the Auditor within 10 days of the ensuing month.
observations.managementAction: No Management action taken; driver’s trip tickets were not in prescribed format and not fully accomplished; Monthly Reports were not prepared/submitted.
observations.reason: Continuous non-compliance; no action taken to prepare monthly reports and to enforce trip ticket requirements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to recognize the RHU II Building and the corresponding accumulated depreciation in the books of accounts with the following journal entry:
Direct the Municipal Administrator to coordinate with the DPWH to obtain the Certificates of Turnover for the two (2) Multi-Purpose Buildings, accepted and signed by the Municipal Mayor. These documents will serve as the basis for recording of the projects, totaling ₱7,917,053.56, in the books of accounts.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Administrator and the Municipal Agriculturist to provide the complete documents necessary for the transfer/donation of assets to the intended beneficiaries, totaling ₱18,068,950.00, to wit:
Approved Sangguniang Bayan Resolutions
Acknowledgement Receipts with identification documents (Primary ID with three specimen signatures)
Deed of Donation
Direct the Municipal Administrator to ensure that every asset transfer/donation is duly supported by approved SB resolutions, a Deed of Donation, and Acknowledgment Receipts supported by identification documents (if applicable).
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to utilize the Property and Equipment for Distribution account to recognize acquired properties and equipment intended for transfer or donation to beneficiaries.
Require the Municipal Accountant to take-up the proposed correcting entries:
To record the donated assets to various farmers’ associations
To record the donated assets to various farmers’ associations

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to collaborate with the Municipal Engineering Office in attaching the required documents, particularly the As Built Plans, before releasing the final payment on infrastructure projects; and
Instruct the HRMO to assign additional Job Order personnel to assist in sorting supporting documents and additional permanent employees to oversee the complete documentation of monthly financial transaction documents for submission to the audit team.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
Record and update the cash books daily, as required by the MNGAS. This will help maintain accurate and current records of all cash transactions.
In coordination with the Municipal Accountant, reconcile the balances of all cash accounts monthly instead of quarterly. This ensures that discrepancies are identified and corrected promptly.

We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Budget Officer to:
Reflect the original budget amounts of the current year’s appropriation in the SCBAA based on the appropriated amounts in the annual budget of the current year.
Include the additional appropriations for the 20% LDF along with the corresponding utilized amount in CY 2024 in the SCBAA to tally with the utilization report.
Present the accurate amounts of the revenue items in the SCBAA that can be verified with the Statement of Financial Performance.
Properly classify the QRF totaling ₱2,686,051.92 as MOOE under the LDRRMF in the SCBAA to accurately reflect the correct amount of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF transferred to STF in CY 2024.
Enjoin the Management to adhere to the provisions of RA No. 7160 regarding the reversion of unexpended balances of MOOE and the proper classification of budgetary items as continuing appropriations.
Immediately revert the remaining balance of MOOE under continuing appropriations for CY 2024 to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund.
Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to present only budget amounts for CO under continuing appropriations and ensure that the amount presented in the SCBAA corroborates with the existing records by using the formula for computation for proper checks and balances.
We further recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to accurately reflect the proper reconciliation of the Capital Outlay in the SCBAA, addressing the discrepancy of the Capital Expenditures with the SCF amounting to ₱2,135,389.60.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to refrain the advance remittance of Provincial and Barangay Shares from the collection of advance payments of taxes to avoid the non-reconciliation of Deferred RPT and SET accounts.
We also recommended the Management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Record the total amount of collected advance payments of taxes under the Deferred RPT and SET accounts and to record the Due to LGUs when they become due and to avoid misstatements on the deferred and liability accounts; and
Revert the unutilized amount of ₱450.55 under Due to Other Funds in the Trust Fund books to the unappropriated surplus in the General Fund books.

We recommended that Management instruct the MDRRMO to ensure the proper programming of Programs, Projects, and Activities by indicating the appropriate expense classification in the LDRRMFIP, in accordance with Section 306 of Republic Act No. 7160.
We also recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Reconcile the recorded balances of the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF and the Cash in Bank-LCCA account under the Trust Fund, making the necessary adjustments with the corresponding supporting documents.
Maintain subsidiary ledgers for each year to facilitate monitoring the balances of the Trust Liabilities-LDRRMF, which should be disclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements.
Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to revert the ₱29,049.25 unutilized balances from 2016 and 2018 under Trust Liabilities-DRRMF to the General Fund for other social services after local sanggunian approval.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to:
Exhaust all possible means to determine the payees of the outstanding Accounts Payable totaling ₱458,641.24. However, if documents evidencing the claims against the LGU could no longer be traced, the long-outstanding Accounts Payable should be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund, pursuant to Section 98 of PD No. 1445, with the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan.
Release the payment for bid security and retention money to the concerned contractors provided that documents evidencing the completion of the projects are available.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting journal entries in the books of accounts, to wit:
Trust Fund
General Fund
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the receipt of share from the Lotto Charity Fund in the books of accounts as income using the account title “Share from PCSO;” and
Direct the MPDC and the MBO to program PPAs for the utilization of the funds in conducting charity programs and activities that are consistent with the PCSO line-up of services under the Lotto Charity Fund, to become more effective partners of the national government in extending services to the marginalized sector in the community.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the misstatement, using the following pro-forma entries:
Record the payments for insurance premiums as prepayments under the Prepaid Insurance accounts and to recognize the monthly expiration of the prepayments under the insurance expense to reflect the accurate balances in the financial reports.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal Assessor to prepare an action plan for completing the required documents for the 12 parcels of land, indicating the following:
Documents necessary for the titling
Timeline of activities
Issues/concerns for each lot that caused the delayed in the titling process
Actions to be taken for each identified issue/concern
Direct the Municipal Assessor to coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer regarding the required budget allocation to complete the survey plans necessary for the processing of titles.
The annual budget should include the necessary amount under Capital Outlay for the initiative to obtain land titles for lands that the LGU claims ownership of.
Instruct the Municipal Assessor to legally document the ownership of untitled lots by obtaining a Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) in the name of the Municipal Government of San Jacinto pursuant to Section 39 of PD No. 1445 in order to protect the interest of the LGU and to avoid future potential disputes.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive take immediate action to require the HRMO, OIC-SB, the Municipal Treasurer, and the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the 151 payroll liquidations totaling ₱29,398,187.55 for the period covering August to December 2024 without further delay. Timely submission of the necessary documents will help prevent the issuance of a Notice of Suspension by the Audit Team and ensure compliance with financial reporting requirements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the HRMO in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to immediately implement the payroll releases through ATM as the establishment of the necessary guidelines which includes the completion of documentary requirements such as DTRs and determination of cut-off date are simple procedural matters requiring further study or delay.

We recommended Management the following:
Consider acquiring a biometric machine for LGU personnel to accurately register actual work hours, thus eliminating manual entry errors. This will ensure data accuracy, reduce the risk of manipulation, and enhance payroll computations by providing real-time, reliable information.
Create and enforce clear guidelines and deadlines for submitting DTRs and other payroll-related documentation, while assigning personnel to strictly monitor and ensure timely submissions. This process will help prevent delays, facilitating the prompt processing of payroll and the timely submission of financial accounts.
To avoid further delays, Management should implement “No DTR, No Payment Policy”.

We recommended that the Management:
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to furnish a certified statement detailing the sources of funds required for the SEF budget preparation to the LSB no later than seven (7) days following the approval of the SEF Budget; and
Require the LSB to hold regular monthly meetings to plan for the implementation of the PPAS and its inclusion in the SEF Budget, as mandated by RA 7160.

We recommended Management the following:
For the Bids and Awards Committee, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, to take immediate action to address the noted deficiencies, and ensure that all lacking documents identified in the auditorial review are submitted.
Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to ensure compliance with the documentary requirements specified in COA Circular 2012-001. This will enable the Audit Team to completely evaluate projects and establish the validity, legitimacy, and propriety of the transaction.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the HRMO to identify and assign the necessary personnel for conducting the final inspection and reassessment of infrastructure projects one year after their completion.
Instruct the General Service Officer to ensure that the motor vehicle assigned to the MEO is always available and ready for use when required, facilitating the efficient completion of inspections.
Instruct the Municipal Engineer to assess the status of the infrastructure projects completed in CY 2023, ensuring that all projects meet the necessary conditions for the issuance of the CFA as specified in Section 62.2 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
Ensure that the issuance of the CFA is closely linked to the confirmation of warranty security and that the Municipal Engineer issues the CFA only after confirming that the contractor has provided sufficient security for any potential post-completion defects or failures.
Monitor the warranty security status for all infrastructure projects and ensure that this security is maintained throughout the warranty period to address any defects or structural failures that may occur.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to monitor and follow up on the timely liquidation of cash advances by preparing and sending demand letters, as needed.
Direct the accountable officer/employee to demand immediate refund of the unliquidated cash advance amounting to ₱10,000.00.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that retention money is deducted when the project is not completed satisfactorily and is behind schedule (with negative slippage), based on the Statement of Time Elapsed and Work Accomplished.
Require the Municipal Engineer to provide a copy of the Statement of Time Elapsed and Work Accomplished for each progress billing to the contractors, enabling them to determine if they can issue retention money bonds equivalent to 10% of the billed amount for each progress billing without negative slippage, in lieu of withholding the retention money from the amount billed.

We recommended that Management:
Continue to insure the properties of the Municipal Government with the GSIS, and require the MTO, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to further identify the properties that need to be insured by preparing the Property Inventory Form (PIF) and submitting it to the GSIS as the basis for the assessment of general insurance coverage for all insurable assets, to provide a safeguard against substantial losses in the event of damage to, or loss of, properties caused by fortuitous events.
Submit a complete inventory of insurable properties to GSIS for them to make an appropriate evaluation of whether the items included can be covered. The annual report must also be submitted to the Audit Team.
Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to provide an appropriation for the payment of premiums for all insurable properties of the Municipal Government.
Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to locate the amounts of the insured properties in the books of accounts to properly determine the remaining uninsured properties.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to post the Notice of Delinquency pursuant to Section 254 of RA 7160.
Require the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Administrator, to devise and implement effective collection strategies for delinquent taxes. These strategies should also encourage taxpayers to pay their taxes annually and help prevent future delinquencies. This may include monitoring the receipt of demand letters and notices of delinquency by each delinquent taxpayer.
Manhours that can be saved from the implementation of the ATM payroll from the existing manual payroll could be used for tax mapping activities.
Instruct the Municipal Administrator, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to enforce administrative and judicial actions within the five-year collection period to all taxpayers with delinquent accounts.

We recommended Management to prepare and approved a memo indicating the deadline of submission of the proposed PPAs by various offices to meet the prescribed date of submission of the GPB.
We further recommended that Management instruct the Chairperson of the GAD Focal Point System-TWG to:
Closely coordinate with the department heads involved in the GPB preparation to properly program GAD-related PPAs by conducting meetings involving the determination of gender issues and how to specifically address them.
Refrain from copying PPAs in the Annual Investment Plan that are not gender-related to properly implement PPAs that will address gender-related issues and promote the empowerment of women and gender equality. This will also prevent inconsistencies between the budgeted amount planned in the GPB for each PPA and the allocated amount in the AIP.
Monitor the utilization and implementation of each GAD PPA to avoid the charging of non-GAD related expenses and to address any lapses in the implementation of GAD PPAs.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Administrator to follow up on the request of the LGU regarding the turnover of the RHU II facility to the Provincial Government.
Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Administrator to coordinate with the donor to determine the market values or costs of the donated items intended for use in the RHU II facility, for proper recording in the books of accounts. Also, the donated items should be disclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements, even if the value to be reflected has not yet been finalized.
Instruct the Municipal Health Officer to designate personnel who will regularly report to the RHU II to ensure utilization of the building while awaiting the response of the Provincial Government.
Require the Municipal Engineer, in coordination with the Municipal Administrator, to set a meeting with the DPWH to determine concrete plans and establish a timeline for the completion and operability of the San Jacinto Eco-park.
Ensure the establishment of electricity connection and water connections for the Pasalubong Center as immediate steps toward the full implementation of the project.
Direct the Municipal Agriculture Office to assign personnel to regularly supervise and maintain the greenhouse facility to prevent further deterioration, address future issues, and ensure full functionality.
Immediately replace the damaged roofing of the greenhouse to restore its usability
Evaluate the feasibility of projects proposed by NGAs to ensure their viability and prevent them from remaining idle after completion.

We recommended that Management:
a. Create a new Inventory Committee comprised of members who are capable
and with sufficient time to conduct the annual physical inventory count.
Further, direct the Inventory Committee to perform the complete physical
count of PPE in accordance with the procedures stated in the “One Time
Cleansing of PPE”. This includes the initial preparation of PIP to set out the
process, procedures and time frame of physical count. Likewise, prepare the
RPCPPE;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant and the newly designated OIC-GSO to
maintain the PPE Subsidiary Ledger Cards and Property Cards,
respectively; and
c. Require both GSO and Municipal Accountant to conduct reconciliation of
their property records and avail the one-time cleansing of PPE account
balances, strictly in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006,
particularly on the disposition and derecognition of non-existing/missing
PPE and adjust the books accordingly, in order to come up with reliable
PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, conditions and
accountability.

We reiterated our recommendations that the LCE:
a. Direct officers and employees with unliquidated CAs to immediately submit
their liquidation papers, otherwise, may constitute to a valid cause for the
withholding of salary of those officers and employees who were unable to
comply with the requirement;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to closely monitor the liquidation of
outstanding CAs and issue on a regular basis demand letter to submit
liquidation reports immediately. Likewise, fast track the recovery of the
documents relevant to the long outstanding CAs and conduct verification,
analysis and validation of them to determine their settlement/collectability
and pursue collection, refund or liquidation, if warranted;
c. Stop the practice of granting additional CA to those who have not submitted
the supporting documents of their Liquidation Reports and those who have
unliquidated CAs; and
d. Conform with the procedures and requirements set forth in COA Circular
No. 2023-008 and immediately facilitate the request for write-off of dormant
unliquidated CAs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Direct all Collecting Officers to immediately remit unliquidated collections to the MTO.
Concurrently, the MT and LO must also be required to immediately deposit all undeposited
collections to the Authorized Government Depository Bank (AGDB). To expedite this
process, demand letters must be issued to all erring Accountable Officers (AOs), including
former MTs and collectors. Institution of appropriate legal actions as well as exhaustion of
all available administrative remedies are also recommended to ensure prompt settlement of
outstanding balances;
b) Require the Collecting Officers to remit all collections daily and in full amount to the
MTO. For collections received by the MT and LO, considering the cost and distance of travel
which may exceed one day to the AGDB, deposits may be made weekly or as soon as the
total collections reach ₱10,000.00 as prescribed in Section 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382;
c) Require all AOs, especially the MT, to maintain and regularly update their individual cash
books. The Municipal Accountant and the MT must also maintain subsidiary ledgers for each
accountable officer. This requirement enhances transparency, accountability, and simplifies
the reconciliation process; and
d) Require the Municipal Accountant and MT to reconcile any discrepancies in the Cash-Local
Treasury account and promptly make the necessary correcting entries in their respective
books. Furthermore, internal controls over collections and remittances must be strengthened
to prevent the future accumulation of unremitted collections.

We recommended that the LCE:
a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to be mindful in the reconciliation of Cash in
Bank accounts particularly by using the books balances shown in the SL instead of
creating any balancing amounts, and regularly update or maintain a SL for each
bank account; and
b) Require the MT and Municipal Accountant to exert effort in tracing the records
or source documents of the non-moving/dormant Cash in Bank accounts in order
to determine the source of funds so as to settle or remove the same from the
books and revert to the national treasury or to the unappropriated surplus of the
GF of the LGU, as the case may be.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Make immediate adjustment of financial records by: (i) removing the
completed projects from the CIP accounts in the total amount of
₱1,237,098.42 and record the same to the appropriate Infrastructure Assets
account, and (ii) reclassify the misclassified Infrastructure Assets account by
debiting the Flood Control Systems account in the amount of ₱3,566,437.16
and Parks, Plazas and Monuments account of ₱496,748.10 and credit to Road
Networks and Other Structures accounts, respectively;
b. Implement stricter internal controls over the recording and classification of
infrastructure assets. This may involve establishing clear procedures for
asset verification, project completion documentation, timely and proper
recording of transactions, including the SLs, pursuant to COA Circular No.
2015-009; and
c. Regular reconciliation of records must be made by the Municipal Accountant
and the Municipal Engineer relative to the status of ongoing and completed
Infrastructure projects of the LGU so as to properly classify and record in the
books the CIP and completed infrastructure projects to present fairly in the
FS.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries
to correct the accounting error. This involves debiting Accounts Payable and
crediting Due from Other Funds, each for ₱7,500,000.00. To prevent future
errors and ensure the fair presentation of FS, all transactions must be
accurately recorded using the appropriate accounts. A review of the chart of
accounts and accounting procedures is recommended to identify and
rectify any weaknesses in the current system; and
b. Direct the Local Finance Committee to be mindful of the guidelines of the
grants or funds received from the national agencies set out in the DBM LBCs
or Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) and ensure that any funds received
for a specified purpose are immediately transferred to the TF.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to
conduct a comprehensive review and reconciliation of the Municipality’s Inter-
Agency Payable accounts in order to identify and rectify any discrepancies
leading to the current negative balance and ultimately present an accurate and
reliable balance of payable accounts in subsequent FS. Regular reconciliation
of accounts with the GSIS is also recommended to promptly identify and
correct any discrepancies.
Henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant to conduct analysis and review
the billings from GSIS per month as against the withheld amounts per payroll
before the payment/remittance is made in order to avoid occurrence of the
negative balance.

We recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to properly account the acquisition and
issuance of the supplies and materials inventory using the Perpetual
Inventory System and make the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct
balance of the affected inventory and expense accounts; and
b. Create a functional Inventory Committee whose members are capable and
with available time to perform the physical count. Require the Committee
also to promptly prepare and submit the RPCI to the Municipal Accountant
for recording and reconciliation purposes, and to COA for audit
verification.

We recommended that Management require the BAC Chairman to carefully
evaluate infrastructure projects considered for lump sum or lot bidding. Ensure that
similar projects or goods readily available from a single supplier/contractor must be
grouped. Dissimilar projects or those not ordinarily available from one source must
be bid separately. Furthermore, a market study must be conducted to assess the
feasibility and efficiency of lot bidding, ensuring multiple bidder participation.

We recommended that Management:
a. Exercise prudence in the utilization of its LDRRMF, taking into
consideration the existing rules and regulations governing the utilization of the
said fund and require the MDRRMO and Municipal Budget Officer to allocate
funds for the expenses chargeable against MOOE of the MDRRM Office;
b. Ensure that the whole LDRRMF including STF and all PPAs charged
against the LDRRMF are incorporated to and covered by the Municipality’s
LDRRMFIP;
c. Refrain from charging against LDRRMF those expenditures not pertaining
to DRRM or not in accordance with the primary purpose for which the fund
was established. Likewise, submit explanation/justification on the noted
deficiencies; and
d. Direct the MDRRMO to submit diligently to COA the monthly utilization
reports of Municipality’s LDRRMF.

We recommended that Management ensure in the succeeding years the transfer
of actual cash pertaining to the unexpended balances of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE
to the STF be made immediately at year-end. Likewise, transfer immediately to STF
the unutilized balance of CY 2023 DRMMF-MOOE.
Further, direct the Municipal Accountant and MT to revert to the GF the CY 2018
STF balance of ₱1,290,388.61 that has remained unutilized for more than five
years.

We recommended that Management require the concerned municipal officials
and employees to devise measures and exert more effort to expedite the preparation
and submission of the accounts and financial reports, including the supporting
documents and schedules, within the prescribed period. We request that such
activities be given utmost priority for timely and better fiscal reporting.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the mandatory
submission of contracts and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor
within five days after perfection for a timely and complete conduct of auditorial and
technical review.

We recommended that Management to:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that supporting documents are
complete and proper prior facilitating payments of SEF financial
transactions;
b. Ensure that CAs from SEF must be given only if use for specific purpose and
directly related to programs and activities of DepEd Schools. All items of
expenditures must be particularly identified and calculated to ensure that the
amounts to be granted are reasonable, accurate and for specific purpose as
required by law; and
c. Require the Local School Board (LSB) DepEd representative to immediately
liquidate his cash advance/financial assistance relative to SEF expenditures
in order to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension of financial transactions.

We recommended that Management direct the GAD Focal Person and GAD key
personnel to ensure that gender-responsive planning and budgeting are observed by
utilizing GAD-related tools like HGDG on the budget attribution to evaluate gender
responsiveness of PPAs in the GPB as well as in the monitoring and evaluation of the
results in the GAD AR.

We recommended the Management to:

Effect the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts; and

Compute the depreciation of Road Networks in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-008, excluding any salvage/residual value from the Road Network components.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Effect the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts; and
b. Compute the depreciation of Road Networks in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-008, excluding any salvage/residual value from the Road Network components.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to:
Refrain from recording prior period adjustments directly to the Government Equity account. Instead, ensure these adjustments are correctly accounted for in the Statement of Changes in Net Assets/Equity to maintain accuracy and consistency in financial reporting.
Ensure the proper presentation of the Statement of Changes in Net Assets/Equity prepared for each year.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
Enjoin the management to adhere to the provisions of RA No. 7160 regarding the reversion of unexpended balances of MOOE and the proper classification of budgetary items as continuing appropriations;
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to immediately revert the remaining balance of MOOE under continuing appropriations for CY 2023 to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund; and
Require the Municipal Budget Office, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to present only budget amounts for CO under continuing appropriations and ensure that the amount presented in the SCBAA corroborate with the existing records by using the formula for computation for proper check and balance.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a complete yearly report on the Certified List of All Real Property Tax Delinquencies which will serve as the supporting schedule of the RPT and SET Receivable balances every end of the year. Having a comprehensive report, the accuracy and reliability of the RPT and SET Receivable balances in the financial statements can be verified and corroborated; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to trace the variance between the RPT and SET Receivable accounts in the amount of ₱100,708.95.

We recommended and management agreed to:
Coordinate with the Provincial Assessor’s Office with the assistance of the Municipal Assessor to determine the appraised value of the fifteen (15) parcels of land which will be the basis in recording in the books of accounts; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the Land account presented in the financial statements by coordinating with the Municipal Assessor to fairly present the recorded amount for these parcels of land.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to:
Submit the lacking documents as enumerated in the lists provided by the Audit Team.
Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to ensure compliance with the documentary requirements to enable the Audit Team to evaluate projects completely, establish the validity, legitimacy and propriety of the transaction.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Budget Office to prepare OBRs prior to the granting of cash advances for the payment of payroll. This is to prevent unauthorized transactions or disbursements and to ensure efficient liquidation process, avoiding unnecessary delays; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to verify that each disbursement is accompanied by approved OBRs prior to the release of funds.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the HRMO to ensure completeness and timely submission to the Municipal Accountant of the needed documents for the liquidation of payroll such as DTRs, Accomplishment Reports of Job Order personnel, and attendance of the SB to facilitate swift processing of documents for the liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that the LCE:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the difference in the balance of the Cash-Local Treasury account between their Offices, submit their working papers/reconciliation documents to the Office of the Auditor, and prepare necessary correcting entries in the Agency’s books or cashbooks;
Direct the Municipal Treasurer, the Liquidating Officers and the collectors to deposit/turnover all future collections intact and not to retain portion of them pursuant to the aforementioned provisions; and
Strengthen internal control over collections, remittances and deposits and consider the promulgation and implementation of a policy in order to avoid retention of big amounts of money in the hands of the accountable officers.

We recommended that the LCE:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to give preferential attention in the preparation and submission of the required BRS, maintain an updated SL per bank accounts, review the records relevant to the Cash in Bank account and effect necessary adjustments to correct the amount presented in the FS and other documents;
Require the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Source Agencies on the status of fund transfers to the Municipality and close the accounts that have become dormant and revert back to the Source Agency or the Bureau of the Treasury, as the case maybe, if the said accounts represent excess of the expenses for the projects funded by NGAs and/or other Agencies; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to prioritize the reconciliation of the Cash in Bank accounts to come up with the correct balances thereof as presented in the FS.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to strengthen accounting controls over liabilities by conducting reconciliation of records, reviewing thoroughly the schedule of payables due to concerned agencies during each month and ensuring that remittances are based on the actual payroll deductions. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant carefully analyze every transaction before recording them in the books and assign personnel to review the entries made to each subsidiary account with negative balances and effect the necessary adjusting entries.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Analyze the affected accounts to determine to which funds the amounts are due and prepare necessary adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct balances of each account in their respective fund classification;
Prepare and maintain SLs for all the aforementioned reciprocal accounts; and
Conduct periodic reconciliation/analysis, preferably on a quarterly basis, of the reciprocal accounts “Due from/to Other Funds” to ensure that necessary adjustments in the books of accounts are made on time.

We recommended that the LCE:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the MPDC on the status of projects recorded under the CIP accounts, reconcile their respective records and properly account the advance payments pertaining to mobilization fees.
Direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and MPDC to exert more efforts in retrieving the previous years’ records to verify the balances of the CIP accounts; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to continue the preparation and maintenance of SLs and records of the Agency accounts duly supported with complete documents to substantiate the balances in the FS.

We recommended that the LCE:
Consider hiring additional competent staffs and personnel in the Accounting Office to hasten the process of preparation and submission of monthly, quarterly and annual financial reports to the Office of the Auditor; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Budget Officer to be mindful of the deadlines and adhere with the provisions of Sections 70 and 73 of the Manual on NGAS, specifically on the submission of trial balances, FS, SAAOB and other supporting schedules.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Accounting Office all the DVs, official receipts including the corresponding reports of each month on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month and the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Office of the Auditor the paid DVs on or before the 10th day of the ensuing month. Also, require the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the unsubmitted paid DVs with complete supporting documents and the original copy of the cancelled check.

We recommended that Management:
Explain the noted deficiencies in the submitted documents;
Prepare and submit the following:
Supplemental LDIP and AIP which include the projects funded by the LGSF-SBDP NTF-ELCAC;
Quarterly Report on Fund Utilization and Status of Program/Project Implementation using the prescribed format; and
Separate subsidiary ledgers for each beneficiary barangay and projects implemented; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere with the timing on withholding taxes and observe proper recording of accounts and ensure that the submitted DVs are supported with complete and properly filled-out documentary requirements.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with DILG, DOF and DBM JMC No. 1, as follows:
Maximize the utilization of the 20 percent DF and implement the PPAs within the targeted timeframe to achieve the social, economic and environmental outcomes essential in the attainment of the constitutional objective of a desired quality of life for all; and
Formulate well-defined procedures to enhance the monitoring and evaluation activities during project implementation, which includes among others, the coordination with the Municipal Engineer in monitoring the implementation of projects funded from the 20 percent DF to attain the optimal socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes which is the core of the fund allocation.
Moreover, we recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the submitted DVs are supported with complete documentary requirements and direct the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare the RAAO of DF in its prescribed format and observe diligence in posting thereto to ensure proper monitoring of the balances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to adhere with the accrual method of accounting by recognizing income when earned, whether collected or not, pursuant to Paragraph 19 of IPSAS No. 9 and Section 47, Volume I of GAM for LGUs. Thus, the uncollected rental income from delinquent stallholders with a total amount of ₱1,079,740.00 be recognized in the books as debit to Operating Lease Receivable and Credit to Rent Income.

Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to prepare monthly billing statements for stall rentals and submit a summary of the same to the OIC-Municipal Accountant for recording accrued income in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Due to Deferment of the Implementation of COA circular No. 2020-004 dated January 31, 2021.
observations.reason: Due to Deferment of Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for LGUs

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

 Instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to record purchases of supplies and materials as inventory by using the perpetual inventory method except for purchases out of petty cash fund which is to be recorded as an outright expense. Issuance of supplies and materials shall be supported by a Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) which will be consolidated in a Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to be the basis of the OIC-Municipal Accountant to recognize the issuance of supplies in the books. 


Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to conduct periodic physical inventory of supplies and materials semi-annually and prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI). The report shall be submitted to this Office not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that policies and guidelines under COA Circular No. 77-61 be given due regard for proper control and monitoring of vehicle utilization and gasoline consumption which included the submission of Monthly Reports of Official Travels and Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption at the end of every month. Prescribed format of said reports are provided for ready reference.

In addition, the following measures are hereby recommended:

For motor vehicle which is not exclusively used by a government official, it is suggested that each time the vehicle is used, require that the vehicle be returned  in the office  full tank, thus the actual no. of liters consumed for each travel is immediately determined and the same shall be indicated in the Driver’s Trip Ticket; and

Ensure that all Driver’s Trip Ticket are properly numbered and recorded in a logbook for control purposes, and maintain a separate file of  Driver’s Trip Ticket for each vehicle for easy monitoring.

Create a position for the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) to carry out effectively the implementation of programs, projects and activities related to Solid Waste Management to address all issues on solid waste;


Require the Municipal Planning & Development Coordinator Officer to prepare the 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan which shall be submitted to the DENR for approval, and provide sanitary landfill for the disposal of collected wastes to avoid open dumpsite; and 

Enjoin the barangay officials regarding the information dissemination on the importance of segregations of waste at source in their barangays in order to provide a pollution and health hazard-free environment.

Stop the practice of charging expenses not related in addressing gender issues, promote women’s empowerment, and gender equality as provided in the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01, and to charge these expenses to other programs of the LGU, where deemed appropriate.

Require the GAD Focal Point to determine GAD related issues in the locality which is necessary in the formulation of GAD Plan and Budget of the Municipality. 

Ensure the preparation of GAD Accomplishment Report which should be submitted on January or February of the succeeding year to support and document the implementation of GAD- related projects, programs and activities as stated in the GAD Plan and Budget in compliance with the provisions of C.8 and 5.5 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA.

Instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to require the submission of accomplishment reports of Job Order Personnel as basis for payment of wages.  

Require the Acting HRMO to submit copies of contract of services of Job Order Workers to support the payment of wages.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, we recommended that electronic or computerized system in assessment and collection be considered and adopted by the LGU of San Jacinto which is a time and cost efficient scheme in processing transactions. Furthermore, it will prevent/minimize errors and inconsistencies in the assessment and collection of taxes and other revenues of the Municipality, whether intentional or not, thus, it will improve or increase the collection of the LGU.
