observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant will prepare the Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF and the LDRRM Officer will submit the said report to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: The LDRRM Officer prepares only quarterly report on the utilization of the DRRMF, and this is subject to reconciliation with the records of the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU prioritize the preparation and submission of the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan to the National Solid Waste Management Commission for approval, as required under Section 16 of RA 9003 to ensure the efficient management of solid waste generated within its jurisdiction. We also recommended the Municipality to immediately close the LGUs existing controlled dumpsite and construct a sanitary landfill.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality will exert more efforts to fast track the approval of their SWM Plan by the NSWC; and will devise an ordinance for the closure of the controlled dumpsite.
observations.reason: The Solid Waste Management Plan of the Municipality is on its second revision and to be submitted again to the EMB Region V for correction/validation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to judiciously plan the programs/projects/activities to be funded out of the 20% EDF in accordance with the mandate of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 2, 2017 and promptly implement all the programs/projects/activities as embodied in the approved Annual Development Plan in order to optimize the delivery of basic services to the people.

We recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to submit all the paid disbursement vouchers/payrolls and other accounts on time to enable us to make a decision in audit. Strict adherence to the laws, rules and regulation relative to the rendition of accounts must be strictly observed.

We recommended that the Chairperson, GAD Focal Point System submit the agency’s   GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) to the Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO) within the month of January to February of each year to see to it that the GPB of the municipality is aligned with the priorities of the provincial government. Likewise, from the PPDO the GPB must be forwarded to the DILG Regional Office within the month of March of each year for appropriate review and approval. The certificate of approval from the latter will ensure that the programs/projects/activities reflected in the GPB are in accordance with the provisions of the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-001 and other existing regulations/guidelines. 

Furthermore, the corresponding GAD Accomplishment Report must also be submitted to the DILG for the corresponding assessment of the reported PPAs undertaken and for its consolidation to be submitted to the Office of the PCW.

We recommended that the Head of the Agency require the Solid Waste Management Board, the Municipal Planning Office and all the Departments involve in planning and conceptualizing the Solid Waste Management Plan to give their full attention and cooperation in the revision and finalization of the draft Solid Waste Management Plan of the LGU to be able to comply with the requirement of the National Solid Waste Management Commission.

We likewise recommended that the Local Chief Executive appoint a permanent employee who will be in-charge of the operation of the Solid Waste Management Board.
observations.managementAction: The Management will exert more efforts for the approval of the Municipality’s Solid Waste Management Plan.
observations.reason: The Municipality established a Solid Waste Management Board, however the Solid Waste Management Plan of the Municipality is still under second revision.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended requiring the Revenue Collection Officers to strictly turnover all their collections intact and in full amount at the close of the business day to the Municipal Treasurer. Likewise, the collections turned over to the Municipal Treasurer including her collections has to be deposited intact to the bank daily or not later than the next banking day. 

He must likewise closely monitor the turn-over/remittances of collecting clerks to prevent possible misuse/misapplication of cash.
	
We also recommended that Management immediately prepare and send demand letter to the previous and current accountable officers requiring them to fully settle the balance of their accountability.
observations.managementAction: The Management committed to implement the herein stated recommendations in the ensuing year.
observations.reason:
Due to the inability of the Management to demand settlement of the undeposited collections of the previous accountable officers.

The Accountable Officers turnovers all their collections to the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the General Services Officer to submit copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders and their complete supporting documents to the Auditor within five (5) days after their perfection for purposes of review of said contracts and technical evaluation by COA TAS.
observations.managementAction: The Management promised to submit copy of perfected contracts together with the supporting documents within five (5) working days from the execution of contract by the government.
observations.reason:
Due to lack of coordination between the BAC and Accounting Department.

The BAC submits only the Contract Agreement, Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and BAC Resolution of some of the PPAs of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Development Coordinator provide the Auditor with a list of all on-going projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year.

We recommended that the Management require the Local Development Council (LDC) to Submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan and Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the formats prescribed in Annex A and B of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that management strictly require each Department Heads to prepare and submit to the General Services Office their respective PPMPs in order that a reviewed and consolidated APP is formulated by the BAC in accordance with SEC. 7, Rule II of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.

We recommended that Management should undertake measures to ensure that Programs/Projects/Activities funded from out of the 20% Development Fund  be fully implemented as planned to enhance the socio-economic condition of its constituents.

We recommended that the agency create a GAD Focal Point System for better ways of performing its task. The above mentioned guidelines serve as the proper way of structuring the GAD Focal Point System. Roles and responsibilities could have been acted upon, only if the GAD Focal Point System is fully-functional. Also, GFPS shall submit the agency’s   GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) to the Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO) within the month of January to February of each year to see to it that the GPB of the municipality is aligned with the priorities of the provincial government. Likewise, from the PPDO the GPB shall be forwarded to the DILG Regional Office within the month of March of each year for appropriate review and approval. Actual GAD projects and activities should also be aligned with the GAD plan per COA circular 14-001 and Joint Circular Memorandum 2013-01.

Considering the material effect of the doubtful accuracy, validity and existence of the property, plant and equipment in the fair presentation of the financial statements of the local government unit,  we have reiterated our previous years audit recommendation which were seemingly overlooked by Management to implement. Facilitate the creation on an Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of  properties of the municipality. Make use of Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) in the issuance of semi-expendable items (such as punchers, staplers, scissors, etc,) for proper accountability and control. 

During  the physical inventory, ensure that all identified unserviceable property and equipment be reported in the Inventory and Inspection of Report of Unserviceable Property which is the basis of  the Accountant to drop the unserviceable items from the respective PPE accounts and reclassify the same to Other Assets account pending its disposal.

observations.recommendation:
Considering the material effect of the doubtful accuracy, validity and existence of the property, plant and equipment in the fair presentation of the financial statements of the local government unit,  we are reiterated our previous years audit recommendation to strengthen the internal control system of the LGU in property management, to wit:
Facilitate the creation on an Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of  properties of the municipality. The actual count could be undertaken any month during the current  year, but it has to be updated at year-end. A copy of the Inventory Report has to be submitted to COA not later than January 31of each year.
Reconcile the PPE per inventory report with accounting records/reports. Any discrepancies shall b verified and adjusted accordingly.  Recipient of any missing PPEs be held accountable.
Maintain/update the PPE ledger cards and Property Card.
Maintain/update the Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR). It has to be renewed every three years or every time there is a change in custodianship/user of the property.
Affix property tags to each property and equipment during the physical inventory-taking for proper control and monitoring.
Make use of Invenory Custodian Slip (ICS) in the issuance of semi-expendable items (such as punchers, staplers, scissors, etc,) for proper accountability and control.
During  the physical inventory, ensure that all identified unserviceable property and equipment be reported in the Inventory and Inspection of Report of Unserviceable Property which is the basis of the Accountant to drop the unserviceable items from the respective PPE accounts and reclassify the same to Other Assets account pending its disposal.
Submit Monthly Report on PPE Purchases  with the following data to be prepared by a designated Property Officer.
Submit Monthly Report on Semi-Expendable Purchases  with the following data to be prepared by a designated Supply Officer.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the OIC-Municipal                      Accounting Office to consider the following audit recommendations:
Maintain individual subsidiary ledger for all accounts, as required under Sections 111 and 114 PD No. 1445.
Exert diligent efforts to locate the necessary supporting documents to substantiate the LGUs recorded obligation and to determine the exact ageing of the liability accounts.
Reclassify capitation fund and guaranty deposits accordingly.
Balances of fund transfers after the completion of the programs or projects be fully liquidated.
Settle all unremitted collections due to GSIS and  Pag-IBIG,
Distribute the all barangay and provincial share from RPT and SET in accordance with RA 7160.
Justify the negative balance of fund transfer from Department Agriculture Regional Office No. 1 amounting to ₱ 119, 027.77 for the construction of Small Farm Reservoir.
We also recommended that accounts payable which has been outstanding for two years or  more may be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund of the national government provided that there is no actual claim, administrative or judicial has been filed and it is not covered by perfected contracts on record.  However, unliquidated balances of accounts payable under the trust funds shall in no case be reverted unless the purposes for which these funds were created have been accomplished.
Ensure that the Journal Entry Voucher for reversion of liability accounts be properly supported with the following:
List of available records and extent of validation made on the accounts;
Certification and reasons why the books of accounts/records, financial statements/schedules and supporting vouchers/documents cannot be located.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Assessor prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names and the amount due and collectible based on RPTAR/Taxpayer’s index card to provide an accurate and reliable basis in the setting-up of receivables for real property taxes and special education taxes at the beginning of the year for fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.
We also recommended that the Municipal accountant ensure that discounts are properly recorded in the books of accounts and reflected in the financial statements.

Guided by the dispositive portion of the “PDAF Decision,” we therefore reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendations that the unexpended balance of PDAF with a total amount of ₱ 4,297,623.97 be returned to the Bureau of Treasury so that the funds could be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund and augment future legitimate funding requirement of the government.

We recommended that Management instruct the OIC-Municipal Accounting Office to record the purchases of supplies and materials by using the perpetual inventory method except for purchases out of the petty cash fund which is to be recorded as an outright expense. Issuance of supplies and materials shall be supported by a Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) which will be consolidated in a Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI). It shall be the basis of the Accounting Unit to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to recognize the monthly issuance of supplies and materials in the books of accounts.
We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall require periodic physical inventory of supplies or property.  Physical count of inventory items by type shall be conducted semestrally and reported in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI). This shall be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.
For other concerns on supply management and accounting thereof, please be guided by Section 112 to 126 of Volume 1 of the LGU-Manual on the New Government Accounting System pending the implementation of Local Government Accounting Manual.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain individual subsidiary ledger for all accounts, as required under Sections 111 and 114 PD No. 1445. Exert diligent efforts to locate the necessary supporting documents to determine the exact age of the receivable accounts, to identify the government agencies and individuals or entities obliged to liquidate and/or refund unutilized balance of fund transfers and to establish the validity and accuracy of the respective receivable account balances presented in the financial statements.
When these accounts are confirmed to be non-moving for ten (10) years or more, these accounts are considered to be dormant. If the analysis or review of the accounts is not    possible due to the absence of records and documents, please be guided by the guidelines and procedures on the write-off of accounts provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005    dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Assessor to exhaust all possible means to document the legality of ownership of the lots through a Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) in the name of the Municipal Government of San Jacinto in compliance with the provisions of Section 39 of PD 1445 and Section 449, Volume 1 of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should require the Municipal Accountant to transfer 50% of the local road account from the Registry of Public Infrastructure to the General Fund books in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-008.  We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should maintain subsidiary ledgers for the Road Networks account to facilitate reconciliation of inventory of the account.

We recommended that the Accounting Division verify the journal entries made relative to the recognition of completed projects in the books of accounts and make the necessary correcting entries  for fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We, therefore, reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that management strictly comply with the existing laws, rules and regulations pertaining to granting and liquidation of cash advances. It is further recommended that:
No additional Cash Advance shall be given to officials/employees unless the previous cash advances was first settled;
All cash advances shall be supported by an Accountant’s Certification that the requesting party has no existing unliquidated cash advances; and
Withhold the salaries of officials and employees whose cash advance remains unliquidated once efforts yield no encouraging results.

We recommended that the local government unit transfer all unexpended/unobligated balance of the Quick Response Fund and the Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund” (account number 2-04-01-020)  in the Trust Fund book.
The following are the suggested journal entries for the transfer of the unexpended/unobligated balances of the QRF and DRRM-MOOE:
Under the General Fund:
Transfer of Unspent Current Year 			x x x
DRRM Funds to Trust Funds
Cash						x x x
To recognize transfer of unexpended/unobligated balances of the
QRF and DRRM-MOOE to special trust fund account.
Under the Trust Fund:
Cash					    x x x
Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction
and Management Fund				  x x x
To recognize transfer of unexpended/unobligated balances of the
QRF and DRRM-MOOE from General Fund.

We recommended that Management conduct physical inventory of all Property, Plant and Equipment to determine the actual insurable properties of the LGU.  We also recommended that Management submit updated inventory report of properties to the GSIS for appraisal purposes to determine the updated amount of premiums for the insurance of all insurable assets, and allocate additional budget for the payment of insurance premiums, if necessary.

We are reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that management adopt the Online Payroll System which is a time and cost efficient scheme of disbursing payroll to its employees. Likewise, it prevents possible risk such as robbery, hold-up and similar incidents. Considering the benefits it could give to both management and employees, immediate implementation is hereby encourage.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to certify only the loan applications of employees whose salary could accommodate the monthly loan amortization and still have a take home pay of not less than the amount specified in the General Provisions of the General Appropriation Act.
Effective CY 2018, the minimum take home pay is increased to ₱ 5,000.00, so please be guided accordingly.

We recommended that management cause the immediate submission of the following reports and accounts, to wit:
Paid disbursement vouchers (all funds)  -  December, 2017
Duplicate copy of Official Receipts  - December, 2017
Ensure that all accounts and reports for succeeding months be submitted on or before the 10th day of the following month to the Auditor pursuant to Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-06 to enable the conduct of timely audit of transactions and to ensure validity, completeness and accuracy of the recorded transactions.

We reiterated our audit recommendation that the Local Chief Executive should  require: (a) the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to faithfully adhere to the procedures in the establishment, utilization and replenishment as well as liquidation of the Petty Cash Fund  as provided in Section 4.3.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 48 of MNGAS, Vol. I,  (b) the Municipal Accountant ensure that Petty Cash Fund shall be supported by an Obligation Request in the setting-up of Petty Cash at the beginning of the year, and (c) the Municipal Treasurer see to it that expenses incurred and replenishment thereof do not exceed the amount of cash advance under Petty Cash Fund to avoid the recurrence of negative balance of the said account in the financial statements.

We recommended that submission of trial balance on the 20th day of the following month and submission of interim reports on the 20th day after the end of each quarter be observed by the Accounting Office pursuant to with Section 70 and 73, Volume 1 of the MNGAS. Ensure that the necessary attachment to the trial balance be also submitted in compliance with the above provision.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer ensure that provisions of Administrative Order No. 5, series of 2008 of the DSWD are adhered to, which include compliance on proper and complete documentation, before effecting the payment.  We also recommended that management refrain from paying financial assistance for religious and private purposes to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit. The LGU should also formulate a concrete basis/guidelines for the selection and retention of student-individual entitled for educational assistance.
It is also recommended that a separate Petty Cash Fund with a reasonable amount be set up to facilitate the payment of financial assistance for burial, transportation, medical assistance and other emergency needs with an amount not exceeding P3,000.00. Pursuant to the general policies of Administrative Order No. 5, series of 2008, these shall be paid in outright cash directly to the client. It will also eliminate numerous reimbursements of financial assistance.

To strengthen the internal control in the use of government vehicles and to regulate fuel consumption of the municipality, we recommended that at the end of every month, the agency submit to the COA Audit Team Monthly Reports of Official Travels and Monthly Reports of Official Fuel Consumption for audit purposes as required by COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977. In addition, the following measures are hereby recommended:
The agency is encourage to establish a credit line with a certain gasoline station that issue computer-generated official receipt (preferably Shell or Petron Gasoline Station).  This will ensure that bills are based on the actual no. of liters purchased.
For motor vehicle which is not exclusively use by any government official, it is recommended that each time the vehicle is used, require that the vehicle be returned  in the office  full tank, thus the actual no. of liters consumed for each travel is immediately determined and the same shall be indicated in the Driver’s Trip Ticket.
To prevent the occurrence of the aforementioned risks in using manually generated Official Receipts as supporting documents in the reimbursement of gasoline expenses, require the LGU officials/ employees to purchase from a gasoline station that issue computerized receipt except for unavoidable circumstances.
Ensure that all Driver’s Trip Ticket are properly numbered and recorded in a logbook for control purposes. Maintain a separate file of the Driver’s Trip Ticket.
Ensure that dates of travel per Driver’s Trip Ticket are consistent with the dates indicated in the Travel Order and Certificate of Appearance.
The Head of the Agency, OIC-Municipal Accounting Office and Head of the Requesting Unit have to exercise judgment and discretion in determining the reasonableness of the claims.

To address the above concerns in the payment of travelling expenses and to avoid suspensions and disallowance in the future, the following measures are hereby recommended:
Be guided accordingly by the rules and regulations   provided in Executive Order No. 298 in the payment/reimbursements of travelling expenses.
Ensure that the claims are supported with original documents except for those documents that were approved or certified by group.
Take advantage of the new interactive technologies such as internet, cellphones, telephones, e-mails and fax machines to convey communications and to follow up official matters.  These are the fastest way to transmit communication.
In the preparation of a travel order, the last entry must be followed by the word “Nothing Follows” to prevent additional entry of dates of travel.  Any modification shall be countersigned by the approving official.
Ensure that dates of travel are consistent with the Driver’s Trip Ticket, Travel Order and Certificate of Appearance.
The Head of the Agency and the OIC-Municipal Accounting Office have to exercise judgment and discretion in determining the reasonableness of the claims.
Please be reminded that the nature of travel shall cover only those that are urgent and extremely necessary and will involve the minimum expenditure and are beneficial to the agency concerned and/ or the country (Handbook on Government Expenditures-National, pp. 97).

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to refrain from granting Honorarium to COMELEC Election Officer and BIR Collection Agent pursuant to DBM Compensation Policy Guidelines No. 98-1 dated March 23, 1998, to avoid disallowance in audit.
In addition, we recommended that Management cause the refund of the total amount of honoraria granted to the COMELEC Election Officer and BIR Collection Agent which is not in accordance with afore cited DBM guidelines.

We recommended that management limit the hiring of experts to its actual need of consultancy services taking into consideration the specific work requiring highly specialized or technical expertise in a field of special knowledge or training which cannot be provided by the regular staff of the Municipality.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the submission of monthly accomplishment report from the Consultants to support the claim of honorarium.

We recommended the creation of Municipal Solid Waste Management Board and designation of a focal person to carry out effectively the implementation of programs, projects and activities related to Solid Waste Management that is geared towards the preservation and protection of the environment and public health as required under R.A. No.  9003.  We also recommended that management involve the participation of the barangay officials in information dissemination on the importance of segregations of waste at source, and to forewarn them that incineration of solid waste  be avoided because it is prohibited by law.

We recommended the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to strictly comply with Section 7 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of RA 9184 and Updated Budget Operations Manual for Local Government Units in the preparation of APP and PPMP.  The BAC must carefully plan and schedule the agency’s procurement in order to manage its resources, produce economical result, and make the procurement procedures more efficient and practical.
Ensure that the prescribed format under DBM Circular Letter 2011-6 be strictly followed in the preparation of APP-CSE  to come up with a realistic budget in the procurement of common use supplies and equipment.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee prepare and submit to the Head of the Procuring Entity the Procurement and Monitoring Report and submit the same to the GPPB pursuant to IRR of RA 9184.
We further recommended that the BAC furnish us copy of the said report for verification and validation purposes on the status of the procurement process. It is also a useful tool for review of contracts related to the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all department heads of the LGU to purchase common-use supplies and equipment at the Procurement Service pursuant to Administrative Order No. 17 s.  2011 and DBM Circular Letter No, 2011-6 to avail the most advantageous price for the government. When goods are to be purchased from local/private suppliers, ensure that these shall always be supported by an evidence of non-availability of stocks in the PS and shall be purchased at reasonable prices.

We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendations that the concerned LGU officials and employees exert extra effort to intensify collection of delinquent accounts and adopt the procedures and remedies provided in Section 254 and 256 of Republic Act No. 7160, otherwise known as the “Local Government Code of 1991, to enable the collections of unpaid local taxes and employ all necessary measures to enable collection of rental from delinquent stall-holders.  These will provide additional funds to support the projected expenditure of the LGU which may be beneficial to its constituents.

We recommended that year-end financial statements and all reports/schedules be submitted within the prescribed period for timely review of reports and for timely submission of Annual Audit Report pursuant to  Section 346, R.A. 7160 of the Local Government Code and Accounting Circular Letter 2007-002 dated January 19, 2007. Year-end trial balances be also submitted on or before February 14 of the following year of audit pursuant to Section 41(2) of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive has to consider the following actions:
Fill-up vacant positions to optimize the utilization of approved budget for Personal Services so that funds shall be utilized for its intended purpose. Initially, require the Head of HRMO to publish the vacant positions in the newspaper as required by the Civil Service Commission, wherein a copy be furnished to this Office.
To unload the Head of the HRMO of additional work and responsibilities, and to be able to submit on time the financial reports and accounts to COA,  the hiring of Municipal Accountant be prioritized. If the ICO Municipal  Treasurer be found qualified for the position of the Municipal Treasurer, she then be encouraged to apply and be considered for the position, considering that her present salary does not match her accountability and function.

Maintain individual subsidiary ledger for all accounts, as required under Sections 111 and 114 PD No. 1445.

  Justify the negative balance of fund transfer from Department Agriculture Regional Office No. 1 amounting to  ₱119,027.77 for the construction of Small Farm Reservoir.

observations.recommendation:
RPT and SET receivables was not set up during 2016, however it was noted that set up receivables in 2013 and 2014  included the amount of delinquent/ previous years tax due which does not conform with Section 20 of  the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) that only the amount due and collectible for the year be set up, hence contributed to the misstatement of the  receivable account.

Total income from the collection of penalties for CY 2016 were not  properly presented in the financial statements due to incorrect accounting journal entry in recording of such transactions in the books of accounts, as a result, the  Tax Revenue-Fines and Penalties-Property Taxes account was understated. Likewise, the balances of RPT/SET Receivable account accounts were also understated by the amount of penalties collected. 

Total discounts granted to taxpayers in CY 2016 were not properly presented in the financial statements due to incorrect accounting journal entry in recording of such transactions in the books of accounts, as a result, the  Discount on RPT-Basic and Discount on SET accounts were understated while the balances of RPT/SET Receivable account were overstated by the amount of discounts given. 

Inaccurate computation of Real Property Tax Receivables, penalties and discounts  resulted to overstatement or understatement of collections from  individual accounts which also affect  the presentation of  total  receivable account  in the financial statement.


We have recommended that the ICO-Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Assessor prepare and furnish the OIC-Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names and the amount due and collectible based on RPTAR/Taxpayer’s index card to provide an accurate and reliable basis in the setting-up of receivables for real property taxes and special education taxes at  the beginning of  the year for fair presentation of  the accounts in the financial statements. Ensure that penalties and discounts are accurately computed and properly recorded in the books of accounts.

of taxpayers.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that claims of Representation and Transportation Allowances be supported with the required documents to be a basis in determining whether the provisions of the afore cited laws, rules and regulations were observed in granting RATA. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer should ensure the claims for RATA are supported with complete documentation before certifying and effecting payment.

We also recommend that a separate payroll shall be prepared for RATA at the end of the month to ascertain the total no. of days work or percentage of actual work/attendance, as the case maybe, to be the basis of its computation.

We have recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer ensure that provisions of Administrative Order No. 5, series of 2008 of the DSWD are adhered to, which include compliance on proper and complete documentation, before effecting the payment.  We also recommend that management refrain from paying financial assistance for religious and private purposes to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit. The LGU should also formulate concrete basis/guidelines for the selection and retention of student-individual entitled for educational assistance.
It is also recommended that a separate Petty Cash Fund with a reasonable amount be set up to facilitate the payment of financial assistance for burial, transportation, medical assistance and other emergency needs with an amount not exceeding P3,000.00. Pursuant to the general policies of Administrative Order No. 5, series of 2008, these shall be paid in outright cash directly to the client. It will also eliminate numerous reimbursements of financial assistance.

However noble the intention of the local government unit in extending financial assistance to the constituents, barangays, schools and various entities in the municipality, compliance with the provisions of existing laws, rules and regulations governing the payment of financial assistance should be observed. We have recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer ensure that provisions of Administrative Order No. 5, series of 2008 of the DSWD are adhered to, which include compliance on proper and complete documentation, before effecting the payment.  We also recommend that management refrain from paying financial assistance for religious and private purposes to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit. The LGU should also formulate concrete basis/guidelines for the selection and retention of student-individual entitled for educational assistance.

should be observed.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that at the end of every month, the agency  submit to the COA Audit Team Monthly Reports of Official Travels and Monthly Reports of Official Fuel Consumption for audit purposes as required by COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977. In addition,  the following  measures  are hereby recommended:

The agency is encourage to establish a credit line with a certain gasoline station that issue computer-generated official receipt (preferably Shell or Petron Gasoline Station).  This will ensure that bills are based on the actual no. of liters purchased. 

For  motor vehicle which is not exclusively use by any government official, it is recommended that each time the vehicle be used, require that the vehicle be returned  in the office  full tank, thus the actual no. of liters consumed for each travel is immediately determined and the same shall be indicated in the 



To prevent the occurrence of  the aforementiond  risks  in using manually generated  Official Receipts as supporting documents in the reimbursement of gasoline expenses, require the LGU officials/ employees to  purchase from a gasoline station that issue computerized receipt except for unavoidable circumstances.

Ensure that  all Driver’s Trip Ticket are properly numbered and recorded in a logbook for control purposes. Maintain a separate file of the Driver’s Trip Ticket.

Ensure that dates of travel per  Driver’s Trip Ticket are consistent with the dates indicated in the  Travel Order and Certificate of Appearance.

The Head of the Agency, OIC-Municipal Accounting Office and Head of the Requesting Unit  have to exercise  judgment and discretion in determining the reasonableness of the claims.
observations.reason: Reiterated under observation no. 19, page no. 63.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to Management the following measures:

Be guided accordingly by the rules and regulations   provided  in  Executive Order No. 298  in the payment/reimbursements of  travelling expenses.
Ensure that the claims are supported with original documents except for those documents that were approved or certified by group.
Take advantage of the new interactive technologies such as internet, cellphones, telephones,      e-mails and fax machines to convey communications and to follow up official matters.  These are the fastest way to transmit communication. 
In the preparation of a travel order, the last entry must be followed by the word “Nothing Follows” to prevent additional entry of dates of travel.  Any modification  shall be countersigned by the approving official.
Ensure that dates of travel are consistent with the Driver’s Trip Ticket, Travel Order and Certificate of Appearance.
The Head of the Agency and the OIC-Municipal Accounting Office have to exercise judgment and discretion in determining the reasonableness of the claims. 
Please be reminded that the nature of travel shall cover only those that are urgent and extremely necessary and will involve the minimum expenditure and are beneficial to the agency concerned.
observations.managementAction: Instructed the concerned personnel to look into the proper computation of per diems and transportation allowance for travel.

We also recommended that management involve the participation of the barangay officials in information dissemination on the importance of segregations of waste at source, and to forewarn them that incineration of solid waste  be avoided because it is prohibited by law.

AAR CY 2015
We recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to submit all the paid disbursement vouchers/payrolls and other accounts on time to enable us to make a decision in audit. Strict adherence to the laws, rules and regulation relative to the rendition of accounts must be strictly observed.

We recommended that the Chairperson,

GAD—Focal—Point—Systemi—submit—the agency’s GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) to the Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO) within the month of January to February of each year to see to it that the GPB of the municipality is aligned with the priorities of the provincial government. Likewise, from the PPDO the GPB must be forwarded to the DILG Regional Office within the month of March of each year for appropriate review and approval. The certificate of approval from the latter will ensure that the programs/projects/activities reflected in the GPB are in accordance with the provisions of the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-001 and other existing regulations/guidelines.

Furthermore, the corresponding GAD Accomplishment Report must also be submitted to the DILG for the corresponding assessment of the reported PPAs  undertaken and for its consolidation to _be submitted to the ~Officeof the PEW-

observations.recommendation:
Facilitate the creation on an Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of  properties of the municipality. The actual count could be undertaken any month during the current  year, but it has to be updated at year-end. A copy of the Inventory Report has to be submitted to COA not later than January 31of each year.
Reconcile the PPE per inventory report with accounting records/reports. Any discrepancies shall b verified and adjusted accordingly.  Recipient of any missing PPEs be held accountable.
Maintain/update the PPE ledger cards and Property Card.
Maintain/update the Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR). It has to be renewed every three years or every time there is a change in custodianship/user of the property.
Affix property tags to each property and equipment during the physical inventory-taking for proper control and monitoring.
Make use of Invenory Custodian Slip (ICS) in the issuance of semi-expendable items (such as punchers, staplers, scissors, etc,) for proper accountability and control.
During  the physical inventory, ensure that all identified unserviceable property and equipment be reported in the Inventory and Inspection of Report of Unserviceable Property which is the basis of the Accountant to drop the unserviceable items from the respective PPE accounts and reclassify the same to Other Assets account pending its disposal.
Submit Monthly Report on PPE Purchases  with the following data to be prepared by a designated Property Officer.
Submit Monthly Report on Semi-Expendable Purchases  with the following data to be prepared by a designated Supply Officer.

Maintain individual subsidiary ledger for all accounts, as required under Sections 111 and 114 PD No. 1445.
Exert diligent efforts to locate the necessary supporting documents  to substantiate the LGUs recorded obligation and to determine the exact ageing of the liability accounts.
Reclassify capitation fund and guaranty deposits accordingly.
Balances of fund transfers after the completion of the programs or projects be fully liquidated.
Settle all unremitted collections due to GSIS and  Pag-IBIG.
Distribute the  all barangay and provincial share from RPT and SET in accordance with RA 7160.
Justify the negative balance of fund transfer from Department Agriculture Regional Office No. 1 amounting to  P119,027.77 for the construction of Small Farm Reservoir.
We have also recommended that accounts payable which has been outstanding for two years or more may be revert to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund of the national government    provided that there is  no actual claim,  no administrative or judicial has been filed and it is not covered by perfected contracts on record.  However, unliquidated balances of accounts payable under the trust funds shall in no case be reverted unless the purposes for which these funds were created have  been accomplished.
Ensure that the Journal Entry Voucher  for reversion of liability accounts be properly supported by the following:
List of available records and extent of validation made on the accounts; and
Certification and reasons why the books of accounts/records, financial statements/schedules and supporting vouchers/documents cannot be located.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Assessor prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names and the amount due and collectible based on RPTAR/Taxpayer’s index card to provide an accurate and reliable basis in the setting-up of receivables for real property taxes and special education taxes at the beginning of the year for fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements. Ensure that penalties and discounts are accurately computed and properly recorded in the books of accounts.

We reiterate our previous year’s audit recommendations that the concerned LGU officials and employees exert extra effort to intensify collection of delinquent accounts and adopt the procedures and remedies provided in Section 254 and 256 of Republic Act No. 7160, otherwise known as the “Local Government Code of 1991, to enable the collections of unpaid local taxes and employ all necessary measures to enable collection of rental from delinquent stall-holders.  These will provide additional funds to support the projected expenditure of the LGU which may be beneficial to its constituents.

We have, therefore, reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendations that the unexpended balance of PDAF with a total amount of P4,297,623.97 be returned to the Bureau of the Treasury so that the funds could be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund and augment future legitimate funding requirement of the government.

We have recommended that Management instruct the OIC-Municipal Accounting Office to record the purchases of supplies and materials by using the perpetual inventory method except for purchases  out of the petty cash fund which is to be recorded as an outright expense. Issuance of supplies and materials shall be supported by a Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) which will be consolidated in a Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI). It shall be the basis of the Accounting Unit to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to recognize the monthly issuance of supplies and materials in the books of accounts.
We further recommend that the Local Chief Executive shall require periodic physical inventory of supplies or property.  Physical count of inventory items by type shall be conducted semestrally and reported in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI). This shall be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.
For other concerns on supply management and accounting thereof, please be guided by Section 112 to 126 of Volume 1 of the LGU-Manual on the New Government Accounting System pending the implementation of Local Government Accounting Manual.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain individual subsidiary ledger for all accounts, as required under Sections 111 and 114 PD No. 1445. Exert diligent efforts to locate the necessary supporting documents  to determine the exact age of the receivable accounts, to identify the government agencies and individuals or entities obliged to liquidate and/or refund unutilized balance of fund transfers and to establish the validity and accuracy of the respective receivable account balances presented in the financial statements.
When these accounts are confirmed to be non-moving for ten (10) years or more, these accounts are considered to be dormant. If the analysis or review of the accounts is not possible due to the absence of records and documents, please be guided by the guidelines and procedures on the write-off of accounts provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Assessor to exhaust all possible means to document the legality of ownership of the lots through a Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) in the name of the Municipal Government of San Jacinto in compliance  with the provisions of Section 39 of PD 1445 and Section 449, Volume 1 of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual.

We have recommended that the Local Chief executive instruct the OIC-Municipal Accounting Office to transfer at least 25% of the local roads from the Registry of Public Infrastructure to the books of the Municipality as part of Property, Plant and Equipment pursuant to provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We, therefore, reiterate our previous year’s recommendation that management strictly comply with the existing laws, rules and regulations pertaining to granting and liquidation of cash advances. It is further recommended that:
No additional Cash Advance shall be given to officials/employees unless the previous cash advances was first settled;
All cash advances shall be supported by an Accountant’s Certification that the requesting party has no existing unliquidated cash advances; and
Withhold the salaries of officials and employees whose cash advance remains unliquidated once efforts yield no encouraging results.

We have recommended that the local government unit transfer all unexpended/unobligated balance of the Quick Response Fund and the Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund” (account number 2-04-01-020)  in the Trust Fund book.
The following are the suggested journal entries for the transfer of the unexpended/unobligated balances of the QRF and DRRM-MOOE:
Under the General Fund Book:
Transfer of Unspent Current Year 			x x x
DRRM Funds to Trust Funds
Cash						x x x
To recognize transfer of unexpended/unobligated balances of the
QRF and DRRM-MOOE to special trust fund account.
Under the Trust Fund Book:
Cash						x	 x x
Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction
and Management Fund					x	 x x
To recognize transfer of unexpended/unobligated balances of the
QRF and DRRM-MOOE from General Fund.

We have recommended that Management conduct physical inventory of PPEs  to determine the actual insurable properties of the LGU.  Submit updated inventory report of properties with the GSIS  for appraisal purposes to determine the updated amount of  premium for the  insurance of all insurable assets. Then,  allocate additional budget for the payment of insurance premium, if necessary.

In view of the foregoing, we are reiterating our previous year’s audit recommendation that management adopt the Online Payroll System which is a time and cost efficient scheme of disbursing payroll to its employees. Likewise, it prevents possible risk such as robbery, hold-up and similar incidents. Considering the benefits it could give to both management and employees, immediate implementation is hereby encourage.

We have recommended that management ensure that all accounts and reports for succeeding months be submitted on or before the 10th day of the following month to the Auditor pursuant to  Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-06  to enable the conduct of timely audit of transactions and to  ensure validity, completeness and accuracy of the recorded transactions.

We have recommended to Management that submission of trial balance on the 20th day of the following month and submission of  interim reports on the 20th day after the end of each quarter  be observed by the Accounting Office  pursuant to with Section 70 and 73, Volume 1 of the MNGAS. Ensure that the necessary attachment to the trial balance be also submitted in compliance with the above provision.

We have recommended that claims of Representation and Transportation Allowances be supported with the required documents to be a  basis in determining whether the provisions of the afore cited laws, rules and regulations were observed in granting RATA. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer should ensure the claims for RATA are supported with complete documentation before certifying and effecting payment.
We also recommend that  a separate payroll shall be prepared for RATA at the end of the month to ascertain the total no. of days work or percentage of actual work/attendance, as the case maybe, to be the basis of its computation.

We have recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer ensure that provisions of Administrative Order No. 5, series of 2008 of the DSWD are adhered to, which include compliance on proper and complete documentation, before effecting the payment.  We also recommend that management refrain from paying financial assistance for religious and private purposes  to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit. The LGU should also formulate a concrete basis/guidelines for the selection and retention of student-individual entitled for educational assistance.
It is also recommended that a separate Petty Cash Fund with a reasonable amount be set up to facilitate the payment of financial assistance for burial, transportation, medical assistance and other emergency needs with an amount not exceeding P3,000.00. Pursuant to the general policies of Administrative Order No. 5, series of 2008, these shall be paid in outright cash directly to the client. It will also eliminate numerous reimbursements of financial assistance.

We have recommended that at the end of every month, the agency  submit to the COA Audit Team Monthly Reports of Official Travels and Monthly Reports of Official Fuel Consumption for audit purposes as required by COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977. In addition,  the following  measures  are hereby recommended:
The agency is encourage to establish a credit line with a certain gasoline station that issue computer-generated official receipt (preferably Shell or Petron Gasoline Station).  This will ensure that bills are based on the actual no. of liters purchased.
For motor vehicle which is not exclusively use by any government official, it is recommended that each time the vehicle is used, require that the vehicle be returned  in the office  full tank, thus the actual no. of liters consumed for each travel is immediately determined and the same shall be indicated in the Driver’s Trip Ticket.
To prevent the occurrence of  the aforementioned  risks  in using manually generated  Official Receipts as supporting documents in the reimbursement of gasoline expenses, require the LGU officials/ employees to  purchase from a gasoline station that issue computerized receipt except for unavoidable circumstances.
Ensure that all Driver’s Trip Ticket are properly numbered and recorded in a logbook for control purposes. Maintain a separate file of the Driver’s Trip Ticket.
Ensure that dates of travel per  Driver’s Trip Ticket are consistent with the dates indicated in the  Travel Order and Certificate of Appearance.
The Head of the Agency, OIC-Municipal Accounting Office and Head of the Requesting Unit  have to exercise  judgment and discretion in determining the reasonableness of the claims.

We have recommended to Management the following measures:
Be guided accordingly by the rules and regulations   provided  in  Executive Order No. 298  in the payment/reimbursements of  travelling expenses.
Ensure that the claims are supported with original documents except for those documents that were approved or certified by group.
Take advantage of the new interactive technologies such as internet, cellphones, telephones, e-mails and fax machines to convey communications and to follow up official matters.  These are the fastest way to transmit communication.
In the preparation of  a travel order, the last entry must be followed by the word “Nothing Follows” to prevent additional entry of dates of travel.  Any modification  shall be countersigned by the approving official.
Ensure that dates of travel are consistent with the Driver’s Trip Ticket , Travel Order and Certificate of Appearance.
The Head of the LGU and the OIC-Municipal Accounting Office have to exercise  judgment and discretion in determining the reasonableness of the claims.

We have recommended the creation of Municipal Solid Waste Management Board and designation of a focal person to carry out effectively the implementation of programs, projects and activities related to Solid Waste Management that is geared towards the preservation and protection of the environment and public health as required under R.A. No.  9003.  We also recommended that management involve the participation of the barangay officials in information dissemination on the importance of segregations of waste at source, and to forewarn them that incineration of solid waste  be avoided because it is prohibited by law.

We have recommended the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to strictly comply with Section 7 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of RA 9184 and Updated Budget Operations Manual for Local Government Units in the preparation of APP and PPMP.  The BAC must carefully plan and schedule the agency’s procurement in order to manage its resources, produce economical result, and make the procurement procedures more efficient and practical.
Ensure that the prescribed format under DBM Circular Letter 2011-6 be strictly followed in the preparation of APP-CSE  to come up with a realistic budget in the procurement of common use supplies and equipment.

We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee prepare and submit to the Head of the Procuring Entity the Procurement and Monitoring Report and submit the same to the GPPB pursuant to IRR of RA 9184.
We further recommend that the BAC furnish us copy of the said report in order for us to verify/validate the status of the procurement process and to be used as basis for our contract review on the procured goods and infrastructure projects.

We have recommended that common-use supplies and equipment be purchased at the Procurement Service pursuant to Administrative Order  No. 17 s.  2011 and DBM Circular Letter No, 2011-6 to avail the most advantageous price for the government. When goods are to be purchased from local/private suppliers, ensure that it shall  always be supported by an evidence of non-availability of stocks in the PS.
We recommended that Management continue with the implementation of  GAD programs and activities in accordance with approved  GAD plans and budget.
We have recommended that management continue to implement BIR rules and regulations to help the government generate more revenues for the benefit of the general public.

We have recommended that management continue to deduct premiums from the salaries and wages of LGU- San Jacinto employees and remit the same with the GSIS pursuant to RA No. 8291 for the social security protections of government workers and employees. Ensure that the amount of P419,622.05 be reconciled with the GSIS for settlement of accounts.

We recommended to require the Municipal Accountant fo submit all the paid
disbursement vouchers/payrolls and other accounts on time to enable us to make a
decision in audit. Strict adherence to the laws, rules and regulation relative to the
rendition of accounts must be strictly observed.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert extra effort to prepare
updated bank reconciliation statements for all funds and to submit the same to COA for
verification. It will be used as basis to ascertain and validate the balances recorded m
the Financial Statements for CY 2016.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management:

a} Strictly enforce the setilement of all unliquidated cash advances as of December
30,2016;

b) Stop the granting of additional cash advances to Accountable Officers, officials
and employees unless the previous ones are fully settled, to avoid accumulation
of the same; and

¢j Institute the appropriate legal actions/sanclions against those erring
accountable officers/employees for continued non-compliance with the existing
laws, rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash
advances, if warranted.

We recommended that the Chairperson, GAD Focal Point Systeml submit the
agency’s GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) to the Provincial Planning and Development
Office (PPDO) within the month of January to February of each year to see (o it that the
GPB of the municipality is aligned with the priorities of the provincial government.
Likewise, from the PPDO the GPB must be forwarded to the DILG Regional Office
within the month of March of each year for appropriate review and approval. The
certificate of approval from the latter will ensure that the programs/projects/activities
reflected in the GPB are in accordance with the provisions of the Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2013-001 and other existing regulations/guidelines.

Furthermore, the corresponding GAD Accomplishment Report must also be
submitted to the DILG for the corresponding assessment of the reported PPAs
undertaken and for its consolidation to be submitted (o the Qffice of the PCW.

We recommended that the Head of the Agency require the Solid Waste
Management Board, the Municipal Planning Office and all the Departments involve in
planning and conceptualizing the Solid Wasie Management Plan to give their full
attention and cooperation in the revision and finalization of the draft Solid Waste
Management Plan of the LGU fo be able to comply with the requirement of the National
Solid Waste Management Commission.

We likewise recommended that the Local Chief Executive appoint a permanent
employee who will be in-charge of the operation of the Solid Waste Management Board.

We recommended requiring the Revenue Collection Officers to strictly turnover
all their collections intact and in full amount at the close of the business day to the
Municipal Treasurer. Likewise, the collections turned over to the Municipal Treasurer
including her collections has to be deposited intact to the bank daily or not later than the
next banking day.

He must likewise closely monitor the turn-over/remittances of collecting clerks to
prevent possible misuse/misapplication of cash.

We also recommended that Management immediately prepare and send demand
letter to the previous and current accountable officers requiring them to fully settle the
balance of their accountability.

We reiterated compliance to the audit recommendations offered in the previous
audit reports which primarily dealt on the assurance of conducting physical count of
LGU properties and submitting updated RPCPPE to the auditor concerned.

We recommended that Management require the General Services Officer to
submit copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders and their complete supporting
documents to the Auditor within five (5) days after their perfection for purposes of review
of said contracts and technical evaluation by COA TAS.

We recommended that the Municipal Development Coordinator provide the
Auditor with a list of all on-going projects/programs/activities and those that are to be
implemented during the year.

We recommended that the Management require the Local Development Council
(LDC) to Submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan
and Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the formats prescribed in
Annex A and B of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We have recommended the following to:

Create an Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of properties of the municipality.

Reconcile the PPE per inventory report with accounting records/reports.

Submit a copy of Inventory Report to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to  the abovementioned regulations.
observations.managementAction:
Currently organizing a committee to conduct physical count/
inventory of all Property, Plant and Equipment of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that all fund transfers received from the National Government Agencies shall accrue to the Trust Fund account as a credit entry to  Project Equity account pursuant to Section 95 of NGAS Manual for Local Government Unit (LGU).
observations.managementAction: Concerned department was advised to transfer all fund from NGAs to Trust Fund account as a credit entry to Project Equity account. Books already adjusted the same.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer exert effort to collect the amount due from the delinquent taxpayers. Formulate plans and adopt policies and strategies by taking into consideration the legal remedies provided under section 174 of RA 7160 to minimize delinquent taxes, if not totally eliminated.
observations.managementAction: The OIC Municipal Treasurer was instructed to double the efforts and formulate plans and strategies to collect the amount due from the delinquent taxpayers.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following to:

Strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances and Section 89 of PD 1445 on the limitations of cash advances.

Cause the immediate liquidation of all outstanding cash advances especially those outstanding for a long period of time. In case the officials and employees concerned fail to liquidate/settle, apply sanctions as provided under Section 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002  and Section 8 Rule V of CSC Resolution No. 04-0676 dated June 14, 2004.
observations.managementAction: All elected/ appointed local officials and employees were advised to liquidate immediately their cash advances before allowing another cash advance.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and collecting officers to comply strictly with the provisions cited in Section 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382 and Section 32 of NGAS Manual, which requires that collections shall be deposited intact with authorized depositories daily or not later than the next banking day.
observations.managementAction: Instructed the ICO Municipal Treasurer to deposit the entire daily revenues and income collections intact with bank not later than the next banking day.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management adopt the Online Payroll System which is a time and cost efficient scheme of disbursing payroll to its employees. Likewise, it prevents possible risk such as robbery, hold-up and similar incidents.
observations.managementAction: Entered  a MOA with Land Bank to install ATM Machine in the municipality and currently in the process of adopting the Online Payroll System.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly enforce the pertinent provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 in the implementation of projects, programs and activities of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Instructed the Bids & Awards Committee to implement and observe the pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 in the implementation of projects of the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management require the project engineers and contractors to  make a signboards for the  on-going projects  pursuant to COA Circular 2013-004 for the purpose of transparency and public information.
observations.managementAction: Instructed all projects engineers and contractors to provide signboards for the on-going projects of the municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entry based on the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) and actual physical count of accountable forms as of a given period to reflect the correct valuation of said inventory account in the financial statement.

For the ensuing purchases of accountable forms, it is recommended that the  accountant recognize an inventory account and expense shall only be recognized on the cost of issued accountable forms.
observations.managementAction: Immediately instructed the concerned department for the adjustment of the books regarding the balance of accountable forms inventory.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following to:

Prioritize the implementation of projects under the GAD plans and programs to attain a desirable socio-economic development and environment outcomes for the general public in accordance with Joint Circular No. 2004-001 dated April 5, 2004.

Submit GAD Plan and Budget and the annual GAD Accomplishment Reports to the Philippine Commission on the Role of Filipino Women and to the DBM.
observations.managementAction:
Instructed the GAD Focal Person to prioritize the implementations of GAD’s plans and programs.
Instructed the GAD Focal Person to do the submission and reporting of the GAD Plans and Programs and other related matters.

observations.recommendation: We  recommended requiring the Revenue Collection Officers to str.‘ctly furnover all their collections intact and in full amount at the close of the business day to the Municipal Treasurer. Likewise, the collections turned over to the Municipal Treasurer including her collections has to be deposited intact to the bank daily or not later than the next banking day. Also, the Audit Team strongly recommended that the Management demand settlement/refund of the undeposited collections of the previous accountable officers.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 12,25 26-27. The Management committed to implement the demand settlement of the undeposited collections herein stated in the ensuing year.
observations.reason: Due to the failure of the Management to the previous accountable officers.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate compliance to the recommendations offered in the previous audit reports which primarily dealt on the assurance of conducting physical count of LGU properties and submitting updated RPCPPE to the auditor concerned.
observations.managementAction: The Management committed  to conduct the annual inventory-taking of all assets or PPEs of the LGU, and submit the RPCPPE and furnish a copy to the Auditor, and reconcile the RPCPPE with the Records.
observations.reason: Lack of coordination among the committee.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly require each Department Heads to Prepare and submit to the General Services Office their respective PPMPs in order that a reviewed and consolidated APP is formulated by the BAC in accordance with SEC. 7, Rule II of the Revised IRR of RA. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Management require each Department Heads to prepare and submit to the General Services Office their respective PPMPs in order that a reviewed consolidated APP will be formulated by the BAC.
